summary: postinstall pipes remote shell script from third-party domain to sh
details: The postinstall script in scripts/postinstall.js (lines 75-78) executes `npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh`. On `npm install`, this unconditionally either installs a global npm package without consent, or — on its failure — fetches a shell script from opencode.ai and pipes it directly to `sh`. The fetched URL is not version-pinned, has no hash or signature verification, and opencode.ai is not the package's own publisher infrastructure (the package is published from github.com/DeadJustLive). Whatever bytes opencode.ai serves at install time execute on the installer's machine with the installer's privileges. The fallback also silently mutates global state by installing `@opencode/cli` globally without prompting. This is the classic install-time-rce shape: mutable remote content piped to a shell from a non-publisher host at lifecycle-hook time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
